intro: Bupleurum chinense, also known as Chinese thunder of heaven, is a traditional medicinal plant used in Asian cultures for its various health benefits. The capsule of Bupleurum chinense contains seeds, which are often used in herbal remedies to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. In traditional Chinese medicine, the capsule is believed to have properties that can help balance and harmonize the body's energy, or "qi."

This tea is said to have anti-inflammatory properties and is believed to help reduce stress and anxiety. It's also used to promote digestion and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes.

side_effects: Bupleurum chinense capsule can have side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. blend recipe: Bupleurum Chinense capsule blend is often combined with other herbs such as Peony root and Licorice root to create a traditional Chinese medicine formula known as Xiao Chai Hu Tang. This blend is commonly used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, as well as to alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety. The typical ratio of ingredients may vary depending on the specific formula and practitioner, but it usually includes 3-6 parts Bupleurum, 3-6 parts Peony, and 3-6 parts Licorice.
recipe: To make bupleurum chinense capsule for medicinal use, the dried root of Bupleurum chinense is typically harvested, cleaned, and processed into a fine powder. The powder is then mixed with a binding agent such as starch or gelatin to form a uniform mixture. The mixture is then filled into gelatin or vegetarian capsules for medicinal use, following proper Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) guidelines.
